run.initial_example:
Concept: Cognitive Bias

Example:
Imagine you are trying to decide which brand of headphones to buy. You read five reviews online. Four of them are glowing, praising the sound quality and comfort. One review is negative, complaining about a broken wire after a week.

Despite the overwhelming positive feedback, the single negative review sticks in your mind. You hesitate, worrying your purchase might also fail. This is an example of the **Negativity Bias** — our tendency to give more weight to negative information than to positive information, even when the positive is more abundant.

Key Insight:
Cognitive biases are systematic patterns of deviation from rational judgment, often shaped by evolution or experience to help us make quick decisions, but they can lead to errors when applied uncritically in modern contexts.
